_note: Updated 2026-05-29 cycle AK overnight-triage. Critic participated tonight via the closing METRIC_INTEGRITY_PROTOCOL § 3.1 attestation AND a substantive independent verification. Tonight was a heartbeat-only cycle (FIQ + bug-reports inbox both ABSENT for the 71st consecutive cycle), so the three § 3.1 concrete questions resolve N/A by absence (verified by directory-absence checks, not waved off): no bug-report diagnoses to scrutinize, no proposals to challenge, no FIQ grades to audit. Cycle AK had a genuine POSITIVE app-health movement (86.9 -> 87.7) -- the PARTIAL self-heal cycle AI projected and cycle AJ doubted. On a positive-movement night the easy spin is the OPPOSITE failure mode from a negative night: claiming a false WIN ('the heartbeat fixed A12' / 'it's resolved now'). The critic guarded against THAT. Independent verification performed: the critic confirmed exactly ONE dimension moved by reading app-health.json verbatim -- A12_operational 60->75 (yellow), label `pipeline=yellow · 7 recent skip-dirty`; A8_performance HELD at 83 (cycle-AH perf gain still sustained); the other 10 dimensions held byte-identical (A1 80, A2 100, A3 98, A4 93, A5 91, A6 92, A7 100, A9 95, A10 100, A11 88). The critic then DEMANDED surfaced-vs-caused proof and got it, AND caught a subtlety the engineer's first pass could have missed: unlike cycle AJ (86.9 already committed at HEAD), THIS cycle the regen PRODUCED 87.7 (HEAD app-health.html still commits 86.9 from the 17:26:48Z post-commit regen) -- which superficially looks like the heartbeat CAUSED the rise. The critic insisted on tracing WHERE A12's inputs come from: the downloads-watcher logs + approvals-pipeline.json are written by the CONCURRENT watcher cron, NOT by regen; aggregate-app-health.py only READS them. So the recovery was ALREADY in the watcher logs before the 18:01 run -> SURFACED not CAUSED, even though the regen wrote the new number. The critic then GROUNDED the 7/10 by demanding the last-10 watcher logs be listed verbatim: 7 skip-dirty + 3 CLEAN ('no new' 17:30/17:35/17:40Z) -- the 3 clean runs rolling into the window are the mechanical cause of 10/10->7/10 -> A12 60->75. CRITICAL reconciliation the critic forced this cycle: app-health A12 says `pipeline=yellow·7-skip` but live approvals-pipeline.json says `status:red · 4 skips` (18:00:48Z) -- a naive read would call this a data-integrity contradiction (HALT-23-adjacent) and either halt or wave it off. The critic resolved it correctly: two DIFFERENT WINDOWS on the same churn (app-health 10-run rolling ratio 7/10->yellow; pipeline json consecutive-streak 4-in-a-row->red), not a contradiction, not corruption. The critic ALSO refused to let the positive move be called a full resolution: the churn CONTINUES under a NEW concurrent ship (the security/pentest harness re-dirtied the tree for the last 4 watcher runs), so A12 is OSCILLATING with the in-flight ship, not healed -- escalated as a continued-awareness item while confirming NOT authoring a fix is correct (watcher safety feature working as designed; concurrent-process resolution; manufacturing a proposal = ship-count gaming per Rule 2). The critic confirmed the commit used an explicit pathspec capturing ONLY this cycle's own files (NOT the concurrent security/pentest churn) -- index-race discipline. The critic actively guarded AGAINST a false-win claim on a positive night, against mis-calling a two-window metric pair as corruption, AND against manufacturing busywork -- rather than rubber-stamping. Critic token counter at ~146k cumulative since last rest (threshold crossed at cycle AH; +~15k this cycle); status remains active and no rest triggered -- heartbeat-only critic load is genuinely light per-cycle. Critic attests cleanly: substantive heartbeat cycle, ship closes.
substantive_output_at_checkpoint: Critic ran the closing METRIC_INTEGRITY_PROTOCOL § 3.1 substantive-vs-fluff attestation for cycle AK and attested CLEANLY, plus exercised a real verification load on a positive-movement night. Three concrete questions: (1) bug-report diagnoses -- N/A, inbox tree absent (directory-absence verified, not waved off); (2) proposals cite a specific screen/state/edge-case -- N/A, zero authored, and the critic validated that NOT authoring a proposal was correct (A12 60->75 is a concurrent-ship dirty-tree oscillation, not a triage-findable defect); (3) FIQ grades reflect rubric dimensions honestly -- N/A, zero live FIQ entries. Substantive verification: on a night where the negative movement REVERSED into a partial recovery, the critic guarded against the positive-night failure mode (claiming a false win). Read app-health.json verbatim -- exactly ONE dimension moved (A12 60->75, pipeline=yellow·7-skip), A8 HELD at 83, 10 dims held. Caught the surfaced-vs-caused subtlety: unlike cycle AJ the regen PRODUCED 87.7 (HEAD commits 86.9), which LOOKS caused -- but traced A12's inputs to the CONCURRENT watcher cron (logs + approvals-pipeline.json), not regen, confirming surfaced-not-caused. Grounded the 7/10 by demanding the last-10 watcher logs verbatim (7 skip + 3 clean 'no new' 17:30/17:35/17:40Z -> the clean runs rolling in dropped 10/10->7/10). Reconciled the app-health-yellow vs approvals-pipeline-red apparent contradiction as two-different-window metrics (rolling-ratio vs consecutive-streak), NOT data corruption. Refused to call the positive move a full resolution -- the churn continues under a NEW concurrent ship (security/pentest harness), A12 is oscillating not healed -- escalating it as continued-awareness while confirming no-fix-warranted (watcher working as designed; concurrent-process resolution; no ship-count gaming). Critic token counter ~146k cumulative (threshold crossed cycle AH; +~15k this cycle); status active, no rest -- heartbeat-only critic load is light. Not generating volume; distinguishing surfaced-from-caused on a night where the regen wrote the number, and partial-recovery-from-resolved on a positive-movement night.
